metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Power Technology 10 explores energy systems, power generation, transmission, and applications in modern society. Students learn about fossil fuels, renewable energy, electrical power systems, engines, and hydraulics. Through projects and analysis, they understand how energy is produced and used while developing critical awareness of environmental and sustainability considerations.
metadata.general_description: Elective course focused on energy systems, power generation, and sustainable technology.

big_ideas.statement: Energy cannot be created or destroyed—only converted from one form to another—and efficiency matters.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students learn thermodynamic principles that govern all power systems. Understanding energy conversion and losses helps them appreciate why efficiency is important and why some energy systems are better than others.

big_ideas.statement: Power generation and energy systems have significant environmental, economic, and social consequences.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students explore trade-offs in energy choices. No energy source is without impact. Critical thinking about how we generate and use power is essential for sustainable futures.

big_ideas.statement: Renewable energy technologies are available now and improving, but widespread adoption requires technological, economic, and political change.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students learn that transitioning from fossil fuels to renewable energy is technically feasible but faces challenges. They explore real-world examples and potential solutions.

big_ideas.statement: Understanding power systems enables informed decision-making about personal, community, and global energy use.
big_ideas.elaboration: By learning how energy works and its impacts, students can make more thoughtful choices about consumption and can advocate for sustainable energy policies.

I can design energy systems that meet specific requirements
I can evaluate renewable energy options
I can optimize systems for efficiency and sustainability

topics: Life cycle analysis of energy systems
